# Diagram: research/orchestrator/tasks/etl/extract_public_actualtripleg_spark.py


> Auto-generated by Obscura crawlers

## Diagram 1

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start([Start]) --> ParseArgs[Parse Command Line Arguments<br/>DB_HOSTNAME, DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD, DATABASE, table]
    ParseArgs --> InitSpark[Initialize Spark Context<br/>master=yarn]
    InitSpark --> ConfigSpark[Configure Spark Settings<br/>timeouts, legacy options]
    ConfigSpark --> ConnectDB[Connect to PostgreSQL]
    ConnectDB --> GetMaxId[Query max id from table]
    GetMaxId --> CheckMaxId{max_id<br/>retrieved?}
    CheckMaxId -->|Success| UseMaxId[Use retrieved max_id]
    CheckMaxId -->|Exception| DefaultMaxId[Set max_id = 6000000000]
    UseMaxId --> ReadData[Read data from PostgreSQL<br/>100 partitions, partitioned by id]
    DefaultMaxId --> ReadData
    ReadData --> WriteS3v1[Write DataFrame to S3<br/>versioned path]
    WriteS3v1 --> GetLatest[Get latest path<br/>remove version folder]
    GetLatest --> WriteS3v2[Write DataFrame to S3<br/>latest path]
    WriteS3v2 --> End([End])
```

## Diagram 2

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Main
    participant SparkContext
    participant PostgreSQL
    participant S3
    
    Main->>SparkContext: Initialize with YARN master
    Main->>SparkContext: Configure timeouts and settings
    Main->>PostgreSQL: Query max(id) from table
    alt Success
        PostgreSQL-->>Main: Return max_id
    else Exception
        Main->>Main: Set max_id = 6000000000
    end
    Main->>PostgreSQL: Read table data (100 partitions)<br/>lowerBound=1, upperBound=max_id
    PostgreSQL-->>Main: Return DataFrame
    Main->>S3: Write DataFrame to versioned path (overwrite)
    Main->>Main: Calculate latest path (remove version)
    Main->>S3: Write DataFrame to latest path (overwrite)
```

## Diagram 3

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class MainScript {
        +get_latest_path(versioned_path) str
        +main(table) void
    }
    class SparkContext {
        +master: str
        +_conf: SparkConf
    }
    class SQLContext {
        +read
    }
    class DataFrame {
        +write
        +head()
    }
    
    MainScript --> SparkContext: creates
    MainScript --> SQLContext: creates
    SQLContext --> DataFrame: reads data
    DataFrame --> S3: writes parquet
```
